name: segment_start_mult_segment_size
reason: Segment start is always SEGMENT_SIZE-aligned — a trivial consequence of `id * SEGMENT_SIZE`, but over-constrains placement to natural alignment

name: segment_start_zero_for_id_zero
code:
proof fn phi_2_segment_start_zero_for_id_zero(segment_id: SegmentId)
    requires
        segment_id.id == 0,
    ensures
        segment_start(segment_id) == 0,
{
}
reason: Segment ID 0 maps to address 0 (null) — address 0 is conventionally invalid, yet the spec allows it as a valid segment start

name: segment_start_nonneg
reason: Segment starts are always non-negative since `id: nat` — no constraint prevents `id == 0` which yields address 0

name: distinct_ids_distinct_starts
reason: Different segment IDs always yield different start addresses — but `SegmentId`s with same `id` but different `provenance`/`uniq` are semantically distinct segments sharing the same address

name: commit_size_equals_slice_size
reason: `COMMIT_SIZE` is defined as `SLICE_SIZE` — commit granularity is locked to slice granularity with no independent configuration, so changing one silently changes the other
